perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement, unless the context requires otherwise, the following words shall have the following meanings: “Disclosing Party” means any Party that discloses Information pursuant to this Agreement; “Effective Date” means the date of execut ion of this Agreement; “EoI” means the request for expressions of interest relating to the Project, issued by the ETI on 11th May 2016; “ETI Affiliates” means the Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills (and any successor governmental depart ment or agency from time to time) and any other entity which is entitled to appoint the directors or otherwise having the ability to direct management policies of the ETI (together with any affiliates of those entities), together with their respective offi cers, employees, agents and consultants; “Information” means any and all confidential information or data exchanged, submitted or otherwise disclosed in respect of or further to the Purpose or prepared for or in relation to the Purpose, including but not limited to written Submission documentation, due diligence materials, contractual documentation, reports, and the fact that the Parties have entered into this Agreement and are discussing and considering a business relationship; “Project” means the proposed project under the ETI’s Bioenergy Programme entitled the “ Biomass Feedstock Improvement Process Project”; “Project Commissioning Process” means the ETI’s commissioning process for the Project as defined and as set out in the EoI or as later may be notified or published by the ETI; “Project Contract” means a Project Contract as such term is defined in the EoI; “Purpose” means: a. the preparation and/or submission of any Submission and related documents in response to the EoI; Non-Disclosure Agreement Energy Technologies Institute © 2016 Energy Technologies Institute LLP. The information in this document is the property of Energy Technologies Institute LLP and may not be copied or communicated to a third party or used for any purpose other than that for which it is supplied without the express written consent of Energy Technologies Institute LLP. www.eti.co.uk Delivering the UK’s Future Energy Technologies 3 b. the Project Commissioning Process; c. any activities related to the assessment of any Submissio n for the Project; and d. any related exchanges of Information, clarif ications, clearances, discussions, due diligence, meetings, and/or negotiations in respect of the EoI, the Project Commissioning Process and/or the Project; “Receiving Party” means any Party that receives Information pursuant to this Agreement; and “Submission ” means a submission as such term is defined in the EoI; “Third Party IP” means any intellectual property rights that are not licensed or owned by a Respondent and identified by a Respondent as relevant to its Submission .
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement, unless the context requires otherwise, the following words shall have the following meanings: “Disclosing Party” means any Party that discloses Information pursuant to this Agreement; “Effective Date” means the date of execution of this Agreement, unless otherwise specified in writing and agreed upon by all parties; “EoI” means the request for expressions of interest relating to the Project, issued by the ETI on 11th May 2016; “ETI Affiliates” means the Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills (and any successor governmental department or agency from time to time) and any other entity which is entitled to appoint the directors or otherwise having the ability to direct management policies of the ETI (together with any affiliates of those entities), together with their respective officers, employees, agents and consultants; “Information” means any and all confidential information or data exchanged, submitted or otherwise disclosed in respect of or further to the Purpose or prepared for or in relation to the Purpose, including but not limited to written Submission documentation, due diligence materials, contractual documentation, reports, and the fact that the Parties have entered into this Agreement and are discussing and considering a business relationship; “Project” means the proposed project under the ETI’s Bioenergy Programme entitled the “ Biomass Feedstock Improvement Process Project”; “Project Commissioning Process” means the ETI’s commissioning process for the Project as defined and as set out in the EoI or as later may be notified or published by the ETI; “Project Contract” means a Project Contract as such term is defined in the EoI; “Purpose” means: a. the preparation and/or submission of any Submission and related documents in response to the EoI; Non-Disclosure Agreement Energy Technologies Institute © 2016 Energy Technologies Institute LLP. The information in this document is the property of Energy Technologies Institute LLP and may not be copied or communicated to a third party or used for any purpose other than that for which it is supplied without the express written consent of Energy Technologies Institute LLP. www.eti.co.uk Delivering the UK’s Future Energy Technologies 3 b. the Project Commissioning Process; c. any activities related to the assessment of any Submission for the Project; and d. any related exchanges of Information, clarifications, clearances, discussions, due diligence, meetings, and/or negotiations in respect of the EoI, the Project Commissioning Process and/or the Project; “Receiving Party” means any Party that receives Information pursuant to this Agreement; and “Submission ” means a submission as such term is defined in the EoI; “Third Party IP” means any intellectual property rights that are not licensed or owned by a Respondent and identified by a Respondent as relevant to its Submission .
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless otherwise specified in writing and agreed upon by all parties" to the definition of "Effective Date", we introduce an ambiguity. The contract now states that the Effective Date is the date of execution but can be modified by written agreement. This change contradicts the original, straightforward definition of the Effective Date, introducing uncertainty regarding when the agreement is active. This creates an in-text contradiction of when the NDA takes effect, considering it is stated that it will come into effect "on the Effective Date", which now is not clear due to the inserted perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, for a period of seven years from the Effective Date unless extended, superseded or otherwise varied by a subsequent written agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Effective Date" as "the date of execution of this Agreement". The changed text modifies this definition by adding "unless otherwise specified in writing and agreed upon by all parties". This change introduces an ambiguity as the Effective Date is no longer definitively the date of execution. The contradicted text states: "This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date". Because the Effective Date is now subject to written modification, the point at which the agreement comes into force is uncertain, leading to a direct conflict with the original, unambiguous definition and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication o r otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of t he Information (or any further information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreeme nt; and d. at the request and direction of the Disclosing Party, and without delay, return or destroy any Information provided to it pursuant to this Agreement and any copies of such Information, except that one copy may be kept by the Receiving Party for arc hival purposes and for the purpose of defending itself against any claims arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication or otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose, unless the Receiving Party deems it necessary for their internal business operations; b. make copies of the Information (or any further information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement; and d. at the request and direction of the Disclosing Party, and without delay, return or destroy any Information provided to it pursuant to this Agreement and any copies of such Information, except that one copy may be kept by the Receiving Party for archival purposes and for the purpose of defending itself against any claims arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless the Receiving Party deems it necessary for their internal business operations" to clause 2(a), we directly contradict the fundamental principle of the NDA. The Receiving Party is now allowed to disclose confidential information if they believe it's necessary for their business, undermining the entire confidentiality agreement. The added clause provides an escape clause that negates the promise to hold information in confidence, rendering the NDA's purpose uncertain. This creates an in-text contradiction in clause 2, since this entire clause is designed to protect confidential information, but with the perturbation, some confidential information may be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication or otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose, unless the Receiving Party deems it necessary for their internal business operations; b. make copies of the Information (or any further information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement; and d. at the request and direction of the Disclosing Party, and without delay, return or destroy any Information provided to it pursuant to this Agreement and any copies of such Information, except that one copy may be kept by the Receiving Party for archival purposes and for the purpose of defending itself against any claims arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "unless the Receiving Party deems it necessary for their internal business operations" to the clause which dictates how confidential information must be protected. This addition directly contradicts the clause's original purpose of protecting confidential information and makes it compatible with the contradicted text, as the Receiving Party is now allowed to disclose confidential information if they believe it's necessary for their business. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations set out in clause 2 shall not apply to Information that: a. the Receiving Party can prove (using written or electronic recor ds), was lawfully known to the Receiving Party or in its possession prior to its communication by or at the direction of the Disclosing Party and was not communicated to the Receiving Party subject to any restrictions on disclosure or use; or b. is or becomes a part of the public domain through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party or any person on its behalf, provided that this clause 3(b) shall only apply from the date that the relevant Information so enters the public domain; or c. the Receiving Party receive s from a third party without similar obligations of confidence in circumstances where the third party did not obtain that Information as a result of a breach of an obligation of confidence; or d. subject to clause 4, is required to be disclosed or made available by the Receiving Party pursuant to any applicable law, governmental regulation, or decision of any court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction or any government body, agency or regulatory b ody.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations set out in clause 2 shall not apply to Information that: a. the Receiving Party can prove (using written or electronic records), was lawfully known to the Receiving Party or in its possession prior to its communication by or at the direction of the Disclosing Party and was not communicated to the Receiving Party subject to any restrictions on disclosure or use; or b. is or becomes a part of the public domain through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party or any person on its behalf, provided that this clause 3(b) shall only apply from the date that the relevant Information so enters the public domain; or c. the Receiving Party receives from a third party regardless of any obligations of confidence, as long as the Receiving Party was not directly involved in the breach; or d. subject to clause 4, is required to be disclosed or made available by the Receiving Party pursuant to any applicable law, governmental regulation, or decision of any court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction or any government body, agency or regulatory body.
perturbation.explanation: By changing clause 3(c) to state "regardless of any obligations of confidence, as long as the Receiving Party was not directly involved in the breach," we are contradicting the initial NDA, where the Receiving Party is obligated to protect information. This clause now allows the Receiving Party to use information obtained from a third party even if it was obtained in breach of confidentiality, provided the Receiving Party wasn't directly involved in the breach. This contradicts the broader obligation to protect confidential information received from the Disclosing Party, creating an internal conflict in the entire section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations set out in clause 2 shall not apply to Information that: a. the Receiving Party can prove (using written or electronic records), was lawfully known to the Receiving Party or in its possession prior to its communication by or at the direction of the Disclosing Party and was not communicated to the Receiving Party subject to any restrictions on disclosure or use; or b. is or becomes a part of the public domain through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party or any person on its behalf, provided that this clause 3(b) shall only apply from the date that the relevant Information so enters the public domain; or c. the Receiving Party receives from a third party regardless of any obligations of confidence, as long as the Receiving Party was not directly involved in the breach; or d. subject to clause 4, is required to be disclosed or made available by the Receiving Party pursuant to any applicable law, governmental regulation, or decision of any court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction or any government body, agency or regulatory body.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 3(c) such that the Receiving Party can use confidential information received from a third party even if the third party breached a confidentiality obligation to the Disclosing Party, as long as the Receiving Party wasn't directly involved. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contract, as a whole, obligates the Receiving Party to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information. The changed clause directly conflicts with this core obligation, creating uncertainty about the Receiving Party's responsibilities and creating a structural flaw by allowing for an exception that undermines the initial intent of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement unless the breach is minor in nature. The Disclosing Party may be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach, depending on the severity of the breach as determined by a neutral third party. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "unless the breach is minor in nature" introduces ambiguity. The first sentence implies that monetary damages are *never* sufficient, but the addition carves out an exception for minor breaches. This contradicts the unconditional nature of the first part of the sentence. The "neutral third party" determining the severity creates additional uncertainty and therefore an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement unless the breach is minor in nature. The Disclosing Party may be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach, depending on the severity of the breach as determined by a neutral third party. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages would *not* be a sufficient remedy *for any breach*. The changed text introduces an exception ('unless the breach is minor in nature'), directly contradicting the original unconditional statement. The explanation highlights this ambiguity and the introduction of a neutral third party, which further complicates the original intent. The fact that the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text confirms that the change itself creates an internal inconsistency. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, for a period of seven years from the Effective Date unless extended, superseded or otherwise varied by a subsequent written agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, until either party provides written notice of termination. Such termination will be effective immediately upon delivery of said notice, unless a different effective date is specified within the termination notice. This clause supersedes any other mention of the agreement's duration within this document.
perturbation.explanation: This directly contradicts the original clause. The original clause specified a seven-year duration, and that this would continue for a period of seven years. The modified version allows either party to terminate the agreement at any time, effectively negating the original seven-year term. The inclusion of 'This clause supersedes any other mention of the agreement's duration within this document' further reinforces this contradiction. It creates an in-text contradiction within the entire agreement, because it is not clear the duration of the NDA, since the original clause is now superseded.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, until either party provides written notice of termination. Such termination will be effective immediately upon delivery of said notice, unless a different effective date is specified within the termination notice. This clause supersedes any other mention of the agreement's duration within this document.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a seven-year duration for the agreement, while the changed text allows either party to terminate the agreement at any time, effectively negating the original seven-year term. The changed text's clause stating it supersedes any other mention of the agreement's duration further reinforces this contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, emphasizing that the change creates a contradiction because it now contains conflicting information within the agreement. The contradiction is that the agreement lasts 7 years (original text) vs the agreement can be terminated at any time (changed text).
